title: Testing of Total Dissolved Solids in Coffee Samples
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bevhf","text":"Testing the total dissolved solids levels in different coffee samples","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Grind coffee to desired consistency and weigh 20g to put in cafetiere.
Boil kettle with 250ml of deionised water.
Pour boiling water into beaker and allow to cool to 93°C, using a digital thermometer to ensure accuracy of temperature.
Once the water has cooled to the desired temperature, pour into the cafetiere and allow to brew for 4 minutes, using a timer/stopclock.
Once the coffee has brewed, use the cafetiere as stated in the instruction manual, and pour coffee into beaker.
Using a syringe, Take 1ml of the coffee and place in the refractometer, placing the syringe against the walls of the well to cool the sample for better reading.
Take note of the refractometer reading.
Repeat this process for all coffee samples.
creator: Shannon.logan
